page: OVHcloud c3-8 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with four dedicated physical cores using the AMD EPYC Milan processor family. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, this instance allocates one thread per core, ensuring dedicated, non-shared CPU resources for predictable execution. The processor configuration includes 32 KB of L1 instruction cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, supporting cryptographic extensions like AES and SHA_NI, alongside vector extensions such as AVX.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a 100 GB NVMe SSD, offering fast read and write speeds directly attached to the instance. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. The hardware profile lacks a dedicated GPU, making it inapplicable for hardware-accelerated graphics or heavy parallel computing tasks.

In benchmark evaluations, the c3-8 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 10% of its class. Text processing capabilities are also strong, ranking in the top 25% of Geekbench results. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. However, static web serving throughput and prompt processing for medium-sized language models are relatively weak, indicating potential bottlenecks in high-concurrency network I/O or memory-bound inference tasks.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and lean memory-to-core ratio, the c3-8 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not demand massive memory footprints. It is designed for applications such as software compilation, text processing pipelines, single-threaded computational tasks, and lightweight CI/CD runners.
description: OVHcloud c3-8 is a KVM-based compute-optimized server featuring four dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC Milan processor. Operating at 2.3 GHz with an x86_64 architecture, it delivers 8.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance excels in single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top tier of stress-ng benchmarks, and shows strong text processing capabilities. While multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are average, static web serving throughput is weak. This hardware profile provides high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks with low memory requirements, aligning with workloads like software compilation, text processing, and CI/CD pipelines.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-8 is a compute-optimized KVM instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 8 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, offering top-tier single-core CPU performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-8 is a compute-optimized KVM instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 8 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring high single-core performance and fast NVMe storage for processing-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Four dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
8.0 GB of system memory and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Strong text processing capabilities in Geekbench testing
Optimized for software compilation, CI/CD, and compute-bound tasks
